differentials.name: angiotensin-converting enzyme (ACE) elevated in 70% (ACE is a product of macrophages and an indicator for the granuloma burden of the body) DDx:  tuberculosis, leprosy, histoplasmosis, berylliosis, cirrhosis, hyperthyroidism, diabetes

differentials.name: Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL): 20-50% lymphocytes with T-suppressor lymphocytes 4- 20 times above normal
differentials.raw: Bronchoalveolar lavage (BAL): 20-50% lymphocytes with T-suppressor lymphocytes 4- 20 times above normal

context: =  BOECK SARCOID [sarkos, Greek = flesh; sarcoid = sarcoma-like] [Caesar Peter Möller Boeck (1845-1917), Norwegian dermatologist describes skin lesions in 1899] =  immunologically mediated multisystem granulomatous disease of unknown etiology with variable presentation, progression and prognosis characterized by noncaseating nonnecrotic epitheloid cell granulomas Bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy is the most common radiologic finding. Adenopathy in the right paratracheal nodes, left aortic-pulmonary window, and subcarinal nodes can also be seen, often with associated pulmonary infiltrates. Preva
